mnemonics.title: Self-Referential Structure Definition
mnemonics.content: SELF REFERENCES NEED POINTERS - Structure Contains Pointer To Same Type, Not Direct Instance

mnemonics.title: Linked List Node Components
mnemonics.content: DATA NEXT - Always Remember: Data Holds Value, Next Holds Address

mnemonics.title: Insertion at Beginning Steps
mnemonics.content: NEW POINTS TO FIRST, HEAD POINTS TO NEW - New->next = head; head = new;

mnemonics.title: NULL Significance
mnemonics.content: NULL MEANS NO MORE - Last Node's Next = NULL Means End Of List

acronyms.usage: Each element in a linked list is called a node containing data and pointer(s)
Linear list with nodes having one pointer to next node only
List with nodes having two pointers: next and previous
Queue behavior - first node inserted is first to be removed
Stack behavior - last node inserted is first to be removed

visualTips.visualization: Draw a box divided into two sections: LEFT section for data (int, float, etc.), RIGHT section for arrow pointing to another identical box. The arrow represents a POINTER, not a copy of the structure.
Imagine a train engine (head) pulling train cars (nodes). Each car has a hook (next pointer) connecting to the next car. NULL means no more cars - end of train.
Think of treasure hunt clues - each clue (node) tells you where to find the next clue. You start at head, follow the address in each node to find the next one.
Hotel with infinite rooms being built one at a time. Each new guest (node) gets a room, and receives the room number of the next room to be built (pointer).

analogies.analogy: Like a person holding a photograph of another person of the exact same type - they are the same 'type' of entity but a different instance.
Linked list is like a treasure hunt where each clue points to the next location. Array is like houses on a street - you can jump directly to house number 5 because they're all next to each other.
Like a dead end sign on a road. When you reach it, there's no further path to follow.
Like checking if a restaurant has tables available before trying to seat customers - if NULL (full), you can't proceed with serving.
Removing a train car - you need to connect the car before and after the removed car so the train doesn't break apart.
